title: tetracera indica
intro: Tetracera indica, commonly known as the Bunga Rampai or False Dill, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce oxidative stress. Tetracera indica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
